subject: Business Associations
title: Agency — Actual and Apparent Authority
rule_text: Actual authority arises from the principal’s manifestations to the agent, while apparent authority arises from the principal’s manifestations to third parties causing reasonable reliance.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agency — Agent’s Liability
rule_text: An agent is personally liable when acting without authority, when the principal is undisclosed or partially disclosed, or when the agent expressly assumes liability.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agency — Formation
rule_text: An agency relationship is created when a principal manifests assent that an agent act on the principal’s behalf and subject to the principal’s control, and the agent consents.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agency — Ratification
rule_text: A principal may ratify an unauthorized act by knowingly accepting its benefits or affirming the act with full knowledge of material facts.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Agency by Estoppel
rule_text: A person may be estopped to deny agency when the person intentionally or carelessly causes a third party to believe another has authority to act for the person, and the third party justifiably and detrimentally relies on that belief.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Agent Liability for Undisclosed or Partially Disclosed Principal
rule_text: An agent who contracts on behalf of an undisclosed or partially disclosed principal is generally liable on the contract unless the parties agree otherwise.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Agent Liability on Contract for Disclosed Principal
rule_text: An agent who enters a contract on behalf of a disclosed principal is generally not liable on the contract if the agent had authority and the contract indicates representative capacity.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agent's Duty of Care
rule_text: An agent owes the principal a duty to act with the care, competence, and diligence normally exercised by agents in similar circumstances.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agent's Duty of Loyalty
rule_text: An agent owes the principal a duty of loyalty and must act solely for the principal's benefit in matters connected with the agency, avoiding self-dealing, usurpation of business opportunities, undisclosed conflicts of interest, and secret profits.

subject: Business Associations
title: Agent's Duty of Obedience
rule_text: An agent must act within the scope of actual authority and obey the principal's lawful instructions concerning the agency relationship.

subject: Business Associations
title: Amending Articles
rule_text: An amendment to the articles of incorporation generally requires board adoption and shareholder approval, plus filing of articles of amendment with the state, unless a statutory exception permits board-only action.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Apparent Authority
rule_text: Apparent authority arises when a principal's manifestations to a third party would cause a reasonable third party to believe that the agent is authorized to act on the principal's behalf.

subject: Business Associations
title: Articles of Incorporation
rule_text: Articles of incorporation generally must include the corporate name, the number of authorized shares, the street address of the corporation's initial registered office and registered agent, and the name and address of each incorporator, along with any optional provisions.

subject: Business Associations
title: Authorized Shares and Issuance
rule_text: The articles fix the number of shares the corporation is authorized to issue. The board may issue shares up to that number in exchange for consideration approved by the board, subject to statutory requirements.

subject: Business Associations
title: Board Action by Written Consent
rule_text: Directors may act without a meeting if all directors consent in writing or in an electronic record, unless the governing statute provides otherwise.

subject: Business Associations
title: Board Committees
rule_text: The board may delegate many management functions to committees of directors, but certain fundamental decisions, such as declaring distributions or approving major structural changes, may remain reserved to the full board under statute.

subject: Business Associations
title: Board Management Authority
rule_text: The corporation's business and affairs are managed by or under the direction of the board of directors, except as otherwise provided by statute, the articles, or valid shareholder arrangements in special contexts.

subject: Business Associations
title: Board Meetings, Notice, and Quorum
rule_text: A majority of the number of directors fixed by the articles or bylaws generally constitutes a quorum for board action, and directors typically must receive notice of special meetings unless waived. No notice is usually required for regular meetings if the time and place are fixed.

subject: Business Associations
title: Business Judgment Rule
rule_text: The business judgment rule creates a presumption that directors acted on an informed basis, in good faith, and with the honest belief that the action taken was in the corporation's best interests. Courts generally will not second-guess such decisions absent fraud, illegality, conflict of interest, or gross abuse.

subject: Business Associations
title: Bylaws
rule_text: Bylaws are internal rules for managing the corporation and regulating its affairs. They are typically adopted by the incorporators or directors and may later be amended by shareholders or directors as allowed by statute and the articles.

subject: Business Associations
title: Class Voting
rule_text: Certain corporate actions that affect a class of shares differently, such as amendments altering class rights, may require separate class voting by that class in addition to any overall shareholder vote.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Close Corporations and Special Control Devices
title: Close Corporation Agreements
rule_text: Close corporation shareholders may adopt agreements restricting share transfer, allocating management powers among shareholders, resolving deadlocks, or creating buy-sell rights, and such agreements are often enforceable if consistent with statute.

subject: Business Associations
title: Common Veil-Piercing Factors
rule_text: Common veil-piercing factors include undercapitalization, failure to observe corporate formalities, commingling of assets, treating corporate assets as personal assets, overlapping ownership and control, and using the corporation to promote fraud or injustice.

subject: Business Associations
title: Control Limitation
rule_text: Traditionally, a limited partner risked personal liability if the limited partner participated substantially in control of the business and a third party reasonably believed the limited partner was a general partner. Modern statutes usually abolish or greatly narrow this control-based liability rule.

subject: Business Associations
title: Controlling Shareholder Duties
rule_text: A controlling shareholder may owe fiduciary duties to minority shareholders and the corporation when exercising control, particularly in freeze-outs, self-dealing transactions, or oppressive conduct in close corporations.

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporate Adoption of Preincorporation Contracts
rule_text: After formation, a corporation may adopt a preincorporation contract expressly or impliedly, making the corporation liable on the contract, though the promoter remains liable unless there is a novation.

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporate Dissolution
rule_text: A corporation may dissolve voluntarily, administratively, or judicially, followed by winding up and distribution of assets.

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporate Dissolution
rule_text: A corporation may generally dissolve voluntarily by board action followed by shareholder approval and filing articles of dissolution, after which the corporation continues only to wind up affairs, pay creditors, and distribute remaining assets.

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporation — Promoter Liability

subject: Business Associations
title: Corporation by Estoppel
rule_text: Under corporation by estoppel, a person who treated an enterprise as a corporation in dealing with it may be estopped from later denying its corporate existence to impose personal liability on the organizers.

subject: Business Associations
title: Creation of Agency
rule_text: Agency is the fiduciary relationship that arises when a principal manifests assent that an agent shall act on the principal's behalf and subject to the principal's control, and the agent manifests assent or otherwise consents so to act.

subject: Business Associations
title: Cumulative Voting
rule_text: Cumulative voting allows shareholders in director elections to multiply the number of shares owned by the number of directors to be elected and cast the resulting votes for one or more candidates, if authorized by the corporation's governing law or charter.

subject: Business Associations
title: De Facto Corporation
rule_text: A de facto corporation may exist when there is a valid incorporation statute, a good-faith colorable attempt to comply with it, and actual use of the corporate form, protecting participants from personal liability in some jurisdictions.

corrected_buzzwords_json: ["de facto corporation","good-faith attempt","use of corporate form"]

subject: Business Associations
title: Demand Requirement
rule_text: Before filing a derivative action, a shareholder usually must make a written demand requesting that the corporation bring the action, and then wait the required statutory period unless irreparable injury would result or demand is excused under applicable law.

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Direct Action
rule_text: A shareholder may bring a direct action for injuries suffered individually, such as denial of voting rights, denial of inspection rights, or oppression, rather than injuries suffered by the corporation as a whole.

subject: Business Associations
title: Director Conflict-of-Interest Transaction
rule_text: A director's conflicting-interest transaction is generally not automatically void if it is disclosed and approved by disinterested directors or shareholders, or if the transaction was fair to the corporation at the time it was entered.

subject: Business Associations
title: Director Duty of Care

subject: Business Associations
title: Director Duty of Loyalty
rule_text: A director owes the corporation a duty of loyalty and must not engage in self-dealing, usurp corporate opportunities, compete unfairly with the corporation, or otherwise place personal interests above the corporation's interests.

subject: Business Associations
title: Director Liability for Unlawful Distribution
rule_text: Directors who vote for or assent to an unlawful distribution may be personally liable to the corporation for the amount exceeding what could have been properly distributed if they failed to comply with the applicable standard of conduct.

subject: Business Associations

rule_text: A director who is present at a meeting when corporate action is taken is generally deemed to have assented unless the director objects at the beginning of the meeting or promptly upon arrival to holding it or transacting business, or the director votes against or abstains and the abstention or dissent is recorded.

subject: Business Associations
title: Disclosure Requirements
rule_text: A limited partnership must generally comply with statutory naming and filing requirements so that third parties can determine the entity's existence and identify persons with management authority.

subject: Business Associations
title: Dissociation
rule_text: Dissociation is the change in the relationship among the partners caused by a partner's ceasing to be associated in carrying on the business. It may be rightful or wrongful and does not always cause dissolution.

subject: Business Associations
title: Dissolution and Winding Up
rule_text: Dissolution is the event triggering the winding up of partnership business. After dissolution, the partnership continues only for the purpose of winding up, including completing unfinished business, collecting assets, paying creditors, and distributing the surplus.

subject: Business Associations
title: Distributions and Dividends
rule_text: Corporations may make distributions only if solvent, meaning they can pay debts as they come due and assets exceed liabilities.

subject: Business Associations
title: Distributions to Shareholders
rule_text: A corporation may make distributions to shareholders unless, after giving effect to the distribution, the corporation would be unable to pay its debts as they become due in the usual course of business or its total assets would be less than the sum of its total liabilities and any preferential amounts due to superior shares on dissolution.

subject: Business Associations
title: Economic Rights of Limited Partners
rule_text: Limited partners generally have the right to share in profits, losses, and distributions as provided by the partnership agreement, and they may transfer economic interests subject to agreement and statute.

subject: Business Associations
title: Employer Liability for Intentional Torts
rule_text: An employer may be vicariously liable for an employee's intentional tort if the act was within the scope of employment, such as when force is job-related, the act was motivated by a purpose to serve the employer, or the conduct was foreseeable in the employment context.

subject: Business Associations
title: Events Causing Dissolution
rule_text: Dissolution may occur by the partners' will, expiration of a term or completion of an undertaking, an event agreed upon in the partnership agreement, judicial decree, or in some cases certain partner dissociations in an at-will partnership.

subject: Business Associations
title: Exceptions for Independent Contractors
rule_text: A principal may be liable for an independent contractor's torts when the principal is negligent in selection, when the work is inherently dangerous, when a nondelegable duty is involved, or when the principal retains substantial control over the work.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Express Actual Authority
rule_text: An agent has express actual authority when the principal expressly communicates to the agent authority to act on the principal's behalf.

subject: Business Associations
title: Frolic and Detour
rule_text: A detour is a minor deviation from assigned work that remains within the scope of employment, while a frolic is a substantial departure for personal purposes that takes the employee outside the scope of employment until the employee returns to the employer's business.

subject: Business Associations
title: General Partner Liability in LP
rule_text: A general partner in a limited partnership generally has management rights and is personally liable for the obligations of the limited partnership, much like a partner in a general partnership, unless the general partner is itself an entity providing liability protection.

subject: Business Associations
title: General Partnership Formation

corrected_buzzwords_json: ["general partnership","two or more persons","co-owners","business for profit"]

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Implied Actual Authority
rule_text: An agent has implied actual authority to do acts reasonably necessary to accomplish an expressly authorized task or acts customarily associated with the agent's position.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Implied Warranty of Authority
rule_text: An agent who purports to act for a principal impliedly warrants to the third party that the agent has authority. If the agent lacks authority and the principal is not bound, the agent may be liable for breach of that implied warranty of authority.

subject: Business Associations
title: Indemnification and Reimbursement
rule_text: A partnership must indemnify a partner for liabilities incurred in the ordinary course of the partnership's business or for preservation of its business or property, and reimburse a partner for payments made and liabilities incurred in the ordinary course or for preservation of the business or property.

subject: Business Associations
title: Independent Contractor Rule
rule_text: A principal is generally not vicariously liable for torts committed by an independent contractor because the principal lacks the right to control the manner and means of the contractor's work.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal
title: Inherent Agency Power
rule_text: Inherent agency power is an older doctrine under which a principal may be bound by certain acts of an agent that are usual or incidental to the agent's position, even absent actual authority, particularly to protect third parties dealing with an agent of an undisclosed principal.

subject: Business Associations
title: Inspection Rights
rule_text: Shareholders generally have the right to inspect certain corporate records, such as articles, bylaws, minutes, and accounting records, if they comply with statutory procedures and, for some records, show a proper purpose.

subject: Business Associations
title: Judicial Dissolution
rule_text: A court may order dissolution in cases such as deadlock, waste, fraud, illegality, or oppression of minority owners, depending on the entity type and statute.

subject: Business Associations
title: Liability After Dissolution
rule_text: After dissolution, a partnership may be bound by acts appropriate for winding up or by acts that would have bound the partnership before dissolution if the third party lacked notice of the dissolution, subject to statutory filing and notice provisions.

subject: Business Associations
title: Limited Liability Companies (LLCs)
rule_text: LLCs combine limited liability of corporations with pass-through taxation and flexible management governed primarily by an operating agreement.

subject: Business Associations
title: Limited Liability Partnership
rule_text: A limited liability partnership is created by filing the required statement with the state, and its principal effect is that partners are generally not personally liable for obligations of the partnership solely by reason of being partners.

subject: Business Associations
title: Limited Partner Liability
rule_text: A limited partner is generally not personally liable for the obligations of the limited partnership solely by reason of being a limited partner, even if the limited partner participates in management under modern statutes.

subject: Business Associations
title: Limited Partnership Formation
rule_text: A limited partnership is formed by filing a certificate of limited partnership or similar document with the appropriate state office, naming at least one general partner and one or more limited partners.

subject: Business Associations
title: Limited Partnerships (LP and LLP)
rule_text: In a limited partnership, general partners manage and are personally liable, while limited partners have limited liability if they do not participate in control; LLPs limit liability for partnership obligations.

subject: Business Associations
title: LLC — Fiduciary Duties and Management
rule_text: LLC managers and members owe fiduciary duties as defined by statute and the operating agreement, which may modify but not eliminate the duty of loyalty in many states.

subject: Business Associations
title: LLC Dissociation and Dissolution
rule_text: A member may dissociate under the statute or operating agreement, and dissolution may occur upon events specified in the operating agreement, member consent, expiration of duration, judicial decree, or other statutory grounds, followed by winding up of LLC affairs.

subject: Business Associations
title: LLC Fiduciary Duties
rule_text: Depending on the statute and operating agreement, members in a member-managed LLC and managers in a manager-managed LLC generally owe duties of loyalty and care to the LLC and, in some circumstances, to the other members, though the operating agreement may modify these duties within statutory bounds.

subject: Business Associations
title: LLC Formation
rule_text: A limited liability company is formed by filing articles of organization or a certificate of formation with the state, creating a separate legal entity distinct from its members.

subject: Business Associations
title: Management Rights
rule_text: Absent agreement otherwise, all partners have equal rights in the management and conduct of the partnership business, and ordinary matters are decided by a majority of the partners while matters outside the ordinary course generally require unanimous consent.

subject: Business Associations
title: Manager Authority
rule_text: Managers in a manager-managed LLC have actual and often apparent authority to bind the LLC in the ordinary course of the LLC's business, subject to limitations in the operating agreement and statutory notice rules.

subject: Business Associations
title: Manager-Managed LLC
rule_text: In a manager-managed LLC, management authority is vested in one or more managers rather than the members, and ordinary management powers belong to the managers, while members usually act more like passive owners unless the operating agreement provides otherwise.

subject: Business Associations
title: Member Liability
rule_text: LLC members are generally not personally liable for the debts, obligations, or liabilities of the LLC solely by reason of being members or participating in management.

subject: Business Associations
title: Member-Managed LLC
rule_text: In a member-managed LLC, each member has authority to participate in management, and each member is generally an agent of the LLC for matters in the ordinary course of the LLC's activities.

subject: Business Associations
title: Mergers and Consolidations
rule_text: A merger generally requires board approval and shareholder approval by the requisite vote of each constituent corporation, unless a statutory short-form merger or other exception applies. The surviving entity succeeds to the rights and liabilities of the constituents by operation of law.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Close Corporations and Special Control Devices
title: Minority Oppression in Close Corporations
rule_text: Minority oppression occurs when controlling shareholders in a close corporation frustrate the reasonable expectations of minority owners through exclusion from management, withholding distributions, termination of employment, or other unfair conduct. Courts may grant equitable relief, including buyouts or dissolution.

subject: Business Associations
title: No Filing Requirement for General Partnership
rule_text: A general partnership need not file any organizational document to exist; it can arise by operation of law from the parties' conduct.

subject: Business Associations
title: Officer Authority
rule_text: Corporate officers have actual authority as granted by the bylaws, board resolutions, or their office, and may also have apparent authority to bind the corporation in matters ordinary for their position.

subject: Business Associations
title: Officer Fiduciary Duties
rule_text: Corporate officers owe fiduciary duties of care and loyalty similar to those owed by directors, adapted to their role and authority within the corporation.

subject: Business Associations
title: Officer Liability on Corporate Obligations
rule_text: An officer who acts for a disclosed corporation is generally not personally liable on a corporate contract if the officer acts with authority and indicates representative capacity, unless the officer personally guarantees the obligation or lacks authority.

subject: Business Associations
title: Operating Agreement
rule_text: An operating agreement governs the relations among the members and managers and between them and the LLC, including management structure, allocations, distributions, transfer restrictions, and dissolution rights, subject to nonwaivable statutory limits.

subject: Business Associations
title: Parent-Subsidiary Structure
rule_text: A parent corporation that owns a controlling interest in a subsidiary does not automatically become liable for the subsidiary's obligations merely by ownership; each corporation remains a separate legal entity absent veil-piercing, assumption of liability, or another exception.

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Partnership — Formation

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Piercing the Corporate Veil
rule_text: A court may pierce the corporate veil and hold shareholders personally liable when the corporation is used to perpetrate fraud, is inadequately capitalized, or is treated as the shareholders' alter ego, and failing to pierce would sanction unfairness or injustice.

subject: Business Associations
title: Preemptive Rights
rule_text: Preemptive rights give existing shareholders the right to maintain their proportional ownership by purchasing a proportionate share of certain new issuances, but such rights generally exist only if granted by the articles or governing statute.

subject: Business Associations
subtopic: Power of Agent to Bind Principal

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations
title: Principal's Duties to Agent

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Business Associations

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Charitable Trust
rule_text: A charitable trust is a trust created for a charitable purpose benefiting the public or a sufficiently broad segment of the public, such as relief of poverty, advancement of education, religion, health, or other community benefits.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Class Gifts
rule_text: If a gift is to a class, surviving class members generally take the entire gift unless an anti-lapse statute applies to a deceased class member within the statute's protection.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Conscious Presence Test
rule_text: Under the conscious presence test, a person signs in the testator's presence if the testator, through sight, hearing, or general awareness, comprehends that the signing is occurring nearby.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Constructive Trust
rule_text: A constructive trust is an equitable remedy imposed to prevent unjust enrichment when a person acquired property through wrongdoing such as fraud, breach of fiduciary duty, or mistake.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Cy Pres
rule_text: If a charitable purpose becomes unlawful, impossible, impracticable, or wasteful, a court may apply cy pres to modify the trust in a manner as near as possible to the settlor's charitable intent.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Delegation by Trustee
rule_text: A trustee may delegate investment and management functions that a prudent trustee of comparable skills could properly delegate, but must use reasonable care in selecting the agent, setting the scope of delegation, and reviewing the agent's actions.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Dependent Relative Revocation
rule_text: Dependent relative revocation allows a court to disregard a revocation if the testator revoked a will or gift based on a mistaken assumption of law or fact and would not have revoked but for that mistake, provided disregarding the revocation better carries out the testator's probable intent.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Disclaimer
rule_text: A valid disclaimer causes the disclaimant to be treated as having predeceased the decedent for purposes of the disclaimed interest.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Discretionary Trust and Creditors
rule_text: Because the beneficiary cannot compel a distribution from a purely discretionary trust, most creditors likewise cannot compel a distribution, though some exception creditors may reach distributions once the trustee decides to make them or if discretion is abused.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Duress
rule_text: A will or provision is invalid for duress if the testator was forced to make the disposition by a wrongful threat that overcame the testator's free will.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Duty of Impartiality
rule_text: If a trust has multiple beneficiaries, the trustee must act impartially in investing, managing, and distributing trust property, giving due regard to each beneficiary's respective interests.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Duty to Inform and Account
rule_text: A trustee must keep qualified beneficiaries reasonably informed about trust administration and material facts necessary to protect their interests, and must provide accountings as required by statute or trust terms.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Duty to Segregate and Earmark
rule_text: A trustee must keep trust property separate from the trustee's own property and properly earmark it as trust property.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Elective Share
rule_text: A surviving spouse may renounce the will and claim the statutory elective share, which is a forced share of the decedent's estate or augmented estate designed to protect the spouse from disinheritance.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Elements of a Valid Trust
rule_text: A valid trust generally requires a settlor with capacity, present intent to create a trust, identifiable trust property, ascertainable beneficiaries unless the trust is charitable or honorary where allowed, a valid trust purpose, and compliance with any required formalities.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Exceptions to Spendthrift Protection
rule_text: Common exceptions allow claims by child or spousal support claimants, providers of necessities, some governmental claims, and sometimes judgment creditors who provided services to protect the beneficiary's interest, depending on statute.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Exculpatory Clause
rule_text: An exculpatory clause may relieve a trustee of liability for ordinary negligence in some jurisdictions, but it generally cannot excuse bad faith, reckless indifference, or abuse of a fiduciary relationship, and may be invalid if improperly inserted by the trustee.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Exoneration of Liens
rule_text: Under modern law, a specific devisee generally takes devised property subject to any mortgage or lien existing at death unless the will directs exoneration.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Failed Residuary Gifts
rule_text: Under the modern rule, if one share of a residuary gift fails, the other residuary beneficiaries typically take the failed share in proportion to their interests rather than the failed portion passing by intestacy.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Formal Requirements for an Attested Will
rule_text: An attested will generally must be in writing, signed by the testator or by another at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence, and signed by the required number of witnesses within the time and manner required by statute.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Fraud in the Execution
rule_text: Fraud in the execution occurs when the testator is deceived about the nature of the document being signed, such that the testator does not realize it is a will or does not understand its contents.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Fraud in the Inducement
rule_text: Fraud in the inducement occurs when the testator knowingly executes a will but is induced by a knowingly false representation to make a disposition the testator would not otherwise have made.
current_buzzwords_json: ["fraud in the inducement","false representation","would not otherwise have made"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["fraud in the inducement","false representation","would not otherwise have made"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: General and Special Powers
rule_text: A general power of appointment may be exercised in favor of the donee, the donee's estate, the donee's creditors, or the creditors of the donee's estate, while a special power may be exercised only in favor of a limited class excluding those persons.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Half-Blood Relatives
rule_text: In most jurisdictions, half-blood relatives are treated the same as whole-blood relatives for intestate succession, unless a statute provides otherwise.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Harmless Error Rule
rule_text: Under the harmless error doctrine, a document not executed in strict compliance with will formalities may still be treated as a will if there is clear and convincing evidence that the decedent intended the document to constitute the decedent's will or amendment.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Holographic Will
rule_text: A holographic will is generally valid if the material portions are in the testator's handwriting and the document is signed by the testator, if recognized by the jurisdiction.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Homestead, Exempt Property, and Family Allowance
rule_text: Many statutes provide a surviving spouse and sometimes minor children with homestead allowance, exempt property, and a temporary family allowance payable off the top of the estate, often in addition to intestate or elective-share rights.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Honorary Trust
rule_text: An honorary trust is a noncharitable trust without an enforceable human beneficiary, often for a pet or grave maintenance, and is valid only to the extent permitted by statute or modern doctrine.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Incorporation by Reference
rule_text: A separate writing may be incorporated by reference into a will if it existed at the time the will was executed, the will manifests intent to incorporate it, and the writing is sufficiently described to permit identification.
current_buzzwords_json: ["incorporation by reference","existed at execution","intent to incorporate","sufficiently described"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["incorporation by reference","existed at execution","intent to incorporate","sufficiently described"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Insane Delusion
rule_text: A will or provision may be invalid if it was the product of an insane delusion, meaning a false belief to which the testator adheres against all reason and evidence, and the delusion materially affected the testamentary disposition.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Integration of a Will
rule_text: Integration allows all papers present at execution that the testator intended to be part of the will to be treated together as one will.
current_buzzwords_json: ["integration","papers present at execution","intended to be part"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["integration","papers present at execution","intended to be part"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Intent to Create a Trust
rule_text: The settlor must manifest present intent to impose enforceable fiduciary duties on a trustee to hold property for beneficiaries.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Interested Witness
rule_text: At common law, a gift to an interested witness could be void unless there were sufficient disinterested witnesses, but many modern statutes purge only the excess gift or abolish the rule entirely.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Lapse
rule_text: A devise lapses if the beneficiary fails to survive the testator, unless an anti-lapse statute or another doctrine saves the gift.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Lost Will Presumption
rule_text: If a will last known to be in the testator's possession cannot be found at death, there is a rebuttable presumption that the testator destroyed it with intent to revoke.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Modern Per Stirpes
rule_text: Under modern per stirpes, shares are first divided at the nearest generation with at least one living taker, and the descendants of deceased members of that generation take by representation.
current_buzzwords_json: ["modern per stirpes","nearest generation with living taker"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["modern per stirpes","nearest generation with living taker"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: No Further Inquiry Rule
rule_text: If a trustee engages in self-dealing, the transaction is generally voidable by the beneficiaries without any need to prove bad faith or unfairness, unless the transaction was authorized, approved, or otherwise excused.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: No-Contest Clause
rule_text: A no-contest clause provides that a beneficiary who contests the will forfeits benefits under the will, but many jurisdictions refuse to enforce the clause when the contestant had probable cause to challenge the will.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Nonmarital Children
rule_text: A nonmarital child generally inherits from the mother automatically and from the father if paternity is established under the applicable statute, such as by adjudication, acknowledgment, or posthumous proof.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Omitted Spouse
rule_text: A spouse who married the testator after execution of the will and is omitted may receive a statutory intestate share or elective provision unless the omission was intentional, the spouse was provided for outside the will in lieu of testamentary transfer, or the will was made in contemplation of marriage.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Partial Revocation by Physical Act
rule_text: In some jurisdictions, a will may be partially revoked by a physical act performed with intent to revoke that part, but other jurisdictions allow only total revocation by act.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Per Capita at Each Generation
rule_text: Under per capita at each generation, the estate is divided equally among living takers at the first generation with survivors, and the shares of deceased persons at that generation are pooled and divided equally at the next generation.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Pour-Over Gift to Trust
rule_text: Under the Uniform Testamentary Additions to Trusts Act and similar statutes, a will may validly pour property over to an inter vivos trust identified in the will, even if the trust is amendable or revocable and even if unfunded during life.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Power of Appointment
rule_text: A power of appointment is an authority enabling the donee to designate who will receive certain property interests, subject to the terms of the instrument creating the power.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Presumption of Undue Influence
rule_text: A presumption of undue influence may arise when a beneficiary had a confidential relationship with the testator, actively participated in procuring the will, and unduly benefited from it.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Pretermitted Child
rule_text: A pretermitted child, usually a child born or adopted after execution of the will and unintentionally omitted, may receive an intestate share or the portion provided by statute unless the omission was intentional or the testator otherwise provided for the child outside the will.
current_buzzwords_json: ["pretermitted child","born after execution","adopted after execution","intestate share"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["pretermitted child","born after execution","adopted after execution","intestate share"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Probate of a Lost or Destroyed Will
rule_text: A lost or destroyed will may be admitted to probate if the proponent proves due execution, the contents of the will, and that the will was not revoked, often by clear and convincing evidence.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Prudent Investor Rule
rule_text: Under the prudent investor rule, a trustee must invest and manage trust assets as a prudent investor would, by considering risk and return objectives reasonably suited to the trust, diversifying investments unless special circumstances justify otherwise, and evaluating the portfolio as a whole.
current_buzzwords_json: ["prudent investor rule","risk and return","diversify","portfolio as a whole"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["prudent investor rule","risk and return","diversify","portfolio as a whole"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Remedies for Breach of Trust
rule_text: For breach of trust, a court may compel performance, enjoin breaches, order redress by money damages or restoration of property, trace and recover trust assets, deny compensation, remove the trustee, or impose other equitable relief.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Removal of Trustee
rule_text: A court may remove a trustee for serious breach of trust, lack of cooperation impairing administration, unfitness, unwillingness, persistent failure to administer effectively, or substantial change of circumstances making removal in the beneficiaries' best interests.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Republication by Codicil
rule_text: A valid codicil republishes the will as of the date of the codicil, so the will is treated as reexecuted on that later date for many purposes.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Resulting Trust
rule_text: A resulting trust arises when a trust fails in whole or part or when circumstances show the transferee was not intended to take beneficially, so the property returns to the settlor or the settlor's estate.
current_buzzwords_json: ["resulting trust","trust fails","not intended to take beneficially"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["resulting trust","trust fails","not intended to take beneficially"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revival of a Revoked Will
rule_text: If a later will that revoked an earlier will is itself revoked, the earlier will is revived only if the testator intended revival or if revival is permitted under the applicable statute and supported by the circumstances.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocable Trust
rule_text: Under modern law, a trust is presumed revocable unless the terms provide that it is irrevocable, although older law presumed the opposite in some jurisdictions.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocable Trust as Will Substitute
rule_text: A revocable living trust allows the settlor to retain control during life while providing for management during incapacity and nonprobate transfer at death, often working with a pour-over will.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocation by Physical Act
rule_text: A will may be revoked by a subsequent physical act, such as burning, tearing, canceling, obliterating, or destroying, performed with the intent to revoke by the testator or at the testator's direction and in the testator's presence.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Revocation by Subsequent Instrument
rule_text: A will may be revoked by a later will or codicil executed with testamentary formalities if the later instrument expressly revokes the prior will or revokes it by inconsistency.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Self-Settled Trust
rule_text: Under the traditional rule, if the settlor creates a trust for the settlor's own benefit, the settlor's creditors can generally reach the maximum amount that could be distributed to or for the settlor's benefit, regardless of spendthrift language, subject to domestic asset-protection trust statutes in a few jurisdictions.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Shares of Descendants
rule_text: Descendants take the balance of the intestate estate by representation under the applicable per stirpes, modern per stirpes, or per capita at each generation system.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Signature by Another on Testator's Behalf
rule_text: A will may be signed by another person on the testator's behalf if done at the testator's direction and in the testator's conscious presence or as otherwise allowed by statute.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Slayer Rule
rule_text: A person who feloniously and intentionally kills the decedent is barred from taking from the decedent's estate, and the estate is distributed as though the killer predeceased the decedent.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Spendthrift Trust
rule_text: A spendthrift clause restrains both voluntary and involuntary transfer of a beneficiary's interest, preventing the beneficiary from assigning it and generally preventing most creditors from reaching it before distribution.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Standing to Contest a Will
rule_text: A person has standing to contest a will if the person has a pecuniary interest that would be affected by the probate of the challenged instrument, such as an intestate heir or beneficiary under an earlier will.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Strict Per Stirpes
rule_text: Under strict per stirpes, the estate is divided into shares at the first generational level below the decedent regardless of whether anyone there survives, and descendants of a deceased child take that child's share by representation.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Support Trust
rule_text: A support trust directs the trustee to make distributions as necessary for the beneficiary's support, education, health, or maintenance, and the beneficiary may compel reasonable distributions within that standard.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Surviving Spouse's Share in Intestacy
rule_text: A surviving spouse's intestate share depends on the statutory scheme and whether the decedent is survived by descendants or parents, with the spouse often taking all if all surviving descendants are also descendants of the spouse and no other descendants complicate the distribution.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Testamentary Capacity
rule_text: A testator has testamentary capacity if, at the time the will is executed, the testator is of the required age and understands the nature of making a will, the general nature and extent of the property, the natural objects of bounty, and the disposition being made.

corrected_buzzwords_json: ["testamentary capacity","nature of making a will","property","natural objects of bounty","disposition"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Trust Property
rule_text: A trust must have trust property, also called the res, that is presently identified and transferable to the trustee or held by the settlor as trustee.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Trustee Duty of Loyalty
rule_text: A trustee must administer the trust solely in the interests of the beneficiaries and avoid self-dealing, conflicts of interest, and transactions affected by personal interests unless properly authorized or consented to.
current_buzzwords_json: ["duty of loyalty","solely in the interests of beneficiaries","self-dealing","conflicts"]
corrected_buzzwords_json: ["duty of loyalty","solely in the interests of beneficiaries","self-dealing","conflicts"]

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Trustee Duty of Prudence
rule_text: A trustee must administer the trust with the care, skill, and caution that a prudent person would use in similar circumstances, taking into account the purposes, terms, and beneficiaries of the trust.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Undue Influence
rule_text: A will or provision is invalid for undue influence if the influencer exerted such pressure that it overcame the testator's free will and caused a disposition the testator otherwise would not have made.

subject: Trusts and Estates
title: Void or Invalid Gifts
rule_text: If a gift is void or invalid, the property usually falls into the residue, or passes by intestacy if the residue does not dispose of it.
